description: Knowledge Base + AI Q&A Web App — SQLite notes with Markdown editor and RAG-powered Claude AI chat

userStories.title: SQLite database layer with CRUD operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a database module to persist notes with full CRUD support and a working test suite.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete any existing vitest.config.ts and create vitest.config.mts (ESM-compatible config for vitest 4 + vite 7)
Add 'test': 'vitest run' script to package.json
Create src/lib/db.ts with: getDb(), createNote(), getNote(), getAllNotes(), updateNote(), deleteNote(), searchNotes()
Database creates 3 tables: notes, note_chunks, conversations
Tags stored as JSON strings in SQLite, returned as string[] in Note type
Create src/lib/__tests__/db.test.ts with 7 tests covering all CRUD functions
All 7 tests pass: npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/db.test.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: vitest.config MUST use .mts extension — vite 7 dropped CJS, so vitest.config.ts fails with ERR_REQUIRE_ESM. Files may already exist from a previous attempt: check src/lib/db.ts and src/lib/__tests__/db.test.ts and vitest.config.ts. If vitest.config.ts exists, delete it and create vitest.config.mts instead. The db module uses a singleton with path-change detection to support testing with different DB_PATH env vars.

userStories.title: Notes REST API routes (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need REST API endpoints to manage notes from the frontend.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/notes/route.ts: GET (all notes or search with ?q=), POST (create note)
Create src/app/api/notes/[id]/route.ts: GET (single), PUT (update), DELETE
GET /api/notes returns all notes as JSON array
GET /api/notes?q=term returns filtered notes
POST /api/notes with {title, content, tags} returns created note with status 201
PUT /api/notes/[id] updates note fields
DELETE /api/notes/[id] returns {success: true}
All routes return 404 with error message when note not found
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: params in Next.js App Router dynamic routes are a Promise: use `const { id } = await params` pattern. Import db functions from @/lib/db.

userStories.title: Text chunking utility for RAG pipeline
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a utility to split note content into chunks for embedding and vector search.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/chunker.ts with chunkText(text, options?) function
Options: { maxChunkSize?: number, overlap?: number } with defaults 1000 and 100
Short text (<= maxChunkSize) returns as single chunk
Long text splits on paragraph boundaries (double newlines) and markdown headers
No empty chunks produced
Create src/lib/__tests__/chunker.test.ts with 4 tests
All 4 tests pass: npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/chunker.test.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Split by /\n(?=# |\n)/ regex to handle both paragraph breaks and markdown headers as chunk boundaries.

userStories.title: Embedding module and RAG vector search
userStories.description: As a developer, I need OpenAI embedding generation and cosine similarity search to power the RAG pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/embedding.ts with getEmbedding(text) and getEmbeddings(texts[]) using OpenAI text-embedding-3-small
embedding.ts accepts optional apiKey parameter to override env var
Create src/lib/rag.ts with: cosineSimilarity(), indexNote(noteId, apiKey?), searchSimilar(query, topK, apiKey?)
indexNote() chunks note content, generates embeddings, stores in note_chunks table as BLOB
searchSimilar() generates query embedding, compares with stored embeddings, returns top K chunks with note info
Create src/lib/__tests__/rag.test.ts with 3 tests for cosineSimilarity and vector math (no API calls needed for these unit tests)
All 3 tests pass: npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/rag.test.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Embeddings stored as Float64Array serialized to Buffer/BLOB in SQLite. cosineSimilarity tests can use static vectors without calling OpenAI API. The API key can come from process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY or a passed parameter.

userStories.title: Claude AI wrapper and streaming chat API route
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Claude API wrapper with streaming and a chat endpoint that uses RAG to answer questions.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/claude.ts with streamAnswer(question, chunks, apiKey?) async generator that streams text via Anthropic SDK
streamAnswer() builds context from retrieved chunks and streams Claude's response
Create src/app/api/chat/route.ts POST endpoint
Chat route accepts {question, apiKey?, openaiApiKey?} in request body
Chat route calls searchSimilar() to find relevant chunks
Chat route streams response as Server-Sent Events (SSE) with text chunks
SSE format: data: {type: 'text', content: '...'} and data: {type: 'sources', sources: [...]}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Anthropic SDK's streaming API. SSE response needs Content-Type: text/event-stream header. Sources should include noteId and noteTitle for citation display.

userStories.title: Embed API route to index a note
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an API endpoint to trigger note embedding/indexing for the RAG pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/api/notes/[id]/embed/route.ts
POST /api/notes/[id]/embed triggers indexNote() from rag.ts
Accepts optional {apiKey} in request body to pass OpenAI API key
Returns {success: true, chunks: number} on success
Returns 404 if note not found
Returns 500 with error message on embedding failure
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This route enables the frontend to index notes after saving. The apiKey from request body should be passed to indexNote().

userStories.title: Note list sidebar component with search and CRUD
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all my notes in a sidebar, search them, create new ones, and delete them.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/hooks.ts with useNotes() custom hook (fetch, create, delete, search notes via API)
Create src/components/NoteList.tsx component
NoteList shows search input at top
NoteList shows 'New Note' button
NoteList renders list of notes with title, date, and tag badges
Selected note is highlighted
Each note item has a delete button (trash icon)
lucide-react is already installed — use it for icons
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: lucide-react is already in package.json. shadcn/ui components (Button, Input, Badge, ScrollArea) can be used. The hooks.ts should debounce search queries.

userStories.title: Markdown editor component with Tiptap
userStories.description: As a user, I want a rich text editor for my notes with title, content, and tag management.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @tiptap/react @tiptap/starter-kit @tiptap/extension-placeholder
Create src/components/Editor.tsx component
Editor has a title input field at the top
Editor uses Tiptap for markdown content editing
Editor has tag input that adds tags on Enter key
Editor shows existing tags as removable badges
Editor calls onSave(note) callback when Ctrl+S is pressed or Save button clicked
Editor shows 'last saved' timestamp
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Tiptap starter-kit includes bold, italic, lists, headings, etc. The onSave prop should accept { title, content, tags } object.

userStories.title: AI chat panel component with SSE streaming
userStories.description: As a user, I want to ask questions about my notes and get AI answers with source citations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/ChatPanel.tsx component
ChatPanel shows conversation history (user messages and assistant responses)
ChatPanel has text input and Send button at bottom
ChatPanel streams responses from /api/chat via EventSource or fetch SSE
Streaming text appears in real-time as it arrives
After streaming completes, source notes are displayed as clickable citations
ChatPanel has loading indicator while waiting for response
ChatPanel accepts onSelectNote(noteId) callback to navigate to cited notes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use fetch with ReadableStream for SSE instead of EventSource (POST request). Parse SSE lines with 'data: ' prefix. Sources arrive after text streaming completes.

userStories.title: Main page assembly with three-panel layout
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to show a note list sidebar, editor panel, and toggleable AI chat panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/page.tsx with three-panel layout
Left panel: NoteList sidebar (fixed width ~280px)
Center panel: Editor fills remaining space
Right panel: ChatPanel toggleable with a button in header
Header shows app title 'Knowledge Base' and AI Chat toggle button
Selecting a note in NoteList loads it in Editor
Creating a note creates via API and selects it
Saving a note calls PUT /api/notes/[id] then POST /api/notes/[id]/embed
Deleting a note calls DELETE /api/notes/[id] and clears editor
Editor shown when a note is selected; placeholder shown otherwise
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Wire up all state: selectedNoteId, showChat, notes list. The embed call on save is async and fire-and-forget (don't block the save UX). Use Tailwind flex layout for the three panels.

userStories.title: Settings page for API key management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to store my API keys in the browser so the app can call Claude and OpenAI.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/settings/page.tsx
Settings page has input for Anthropic API key and OpenAI API key
Keys are saved to localStorage on form submit
Show/hide password toggle for each key input
Settings link in main page header (gear icon)
API keys are read from localStorage and passed in request headers or body to API routes
Update src/app/api/chat/route.ts to accept apiKey and openaiApiKey from request body
Update src/app/api/notes/[id]/embed/route.ts to accept apiKey from request body
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Store keys as 'anthropic_api_key' and 'openai_api_key' in localStorage. The frontend reads these and includes them in fetch request bodies.

userStories.title: Environment setup and .gitignore updates
userStories.description: As a developer, I need example environment files and proper .gitignore entries.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .env.local.example with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and OPENAI_API_KEY placeholder values
Add *.db to .gitignore if not already present
Add data/ to .gitignore if not already present
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Simple housekeeping task. Check existing .gitignore before adding entries.

userStories.title: Dark/light theme toggle with next-themes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between dark and light mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Install next-themes
Create src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx wrapping next-themes ThemeProvider
Create src/components/ThemeToggle.tsx with sun/moon icon button
Update src/app/layout.tsx to wrap with ThemeProvider (attribute='class', defaultTheme='system')
Add ThemeToggle to main page header
Tailwind dark mode works (add darkMode: 'class' to tailwind config if needed)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: next-themes works by setting a class on the html element. Tailwind v4 may handle dark mode differently — check tailwind config. Use 'suppressHydrationWarning' on html element in layout.tsx.

userStories.title: Final integration testing and polish
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all tests passing and the full workflow working end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Run npx vitest run — all tests pass
Run npx tsc --noEmit — no type errors
npm run build succeeds without errors
All user stories have passes: true in prd.json
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fix any remaining type errors or test failures. Focus on making build and typecheck pass cleanly.
